message:
fix: Variable shadowing detection for module-level functions

- Add module_level_funcs map to NativeCodegen to track all function names
- Rename function parameters that shadow module-level functions (suffix __local)
- Rename local variables in assignments and for loops that shadow module-level functions
- Handle both regular for loops and tuple-unpacking for loops
- Register renames in var_renames so body references use correct names

+        echo -e "  ${GREEN}✓${NC} metal0: $METAL0_TIME"

-        echo -e "  ${YELLOW}⚠${NC} metal0 compile failed (will skip)"

     fi

@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+# metal0 tokenizer benchmark
+# Apple-to-apple comparison with tiktoken, rs-bpe, HuggingFace
+from metal0 import tokenizer
+import time
+import json
+
+# Init tokenizer
+tokenizer.init("/Users/steven_chong/Downloads/repos/metal0/packages/tokenizer/dist/cl100k_base_full.json")
+
+# Load benchmark data using json.load
+f = open("/Users/steven_chong/Downloads/repos/metal0/packages/tokenizer/benchmark_data.json", "r")
+data = json.load(f)
+f.close()
+
+texts = data["texts"]
+
+# Warmup (5 iterations)
+i = 0
+while i < 5:
+    for t in texts:
+        tokenizer.encode(t)
+    i = i + 1
+
+# Benchmark: 100 iterations (same as tiktoken)
+start = time.time()
+i = 0
+while i < 100:
+    for t in texts:
+        tokenizer.encode(t)
+    i = i + 1
+
+elapsed_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000
+print(elapsed_ms, "ms")
